Personal Details
Name: Fabienne Louise Bruggeman  |  DOB: 16 August 2000  |  Nationality: Dutch
Location: Aldinga Beach, South Australia
Email: fabienne.bruggeman@hotmail.com
Profile
As an educator and recent master's graduate of European Studies, I value translating this blend of practical expertise and academic discipline in results-driven roles. On a personal level, I am driven by the conviction that narrative representation functions as a critical mechanism for social change. Based on that principle, I have experience as an ESL teacher, a student assistant at an educational foundation focused on providing accessible education, and as an intern at the Dutch sustainable financial institution ASN Bank. I currently operate at the intersection of recruiting and AI innovation for Innodata, building bridges across academia and the AI industry to ensure that the development of LLMs is guided by expert-led data. I have also adopted digital product building (SaaS) as a technical pursuit. Over the past years, I have gained experience living in Japan, Australia, Scotland and the Netherlands. I am native in Dutch and English, and speak Japanese at a conversational level.
Education
MA European Studies
University of Amsterdam  |  EQF Level 7
Specialisation in Identity and Integration — European culture and politics, media and identity, race and the state. Thesis research at the Black Cultural Archives, London.

BA English Language and Culture
Utrecht University  |  EQF Level 6
Specialisation in Intertextuality. Minor in Violence (UvA). Semester abroad at Kyoto University. Communications internship at ASN Bank.
Skills
Microsoft Tools • Python • JavaScript • HTML • Vercel • GitHub • Supabase • Writing • Translation • Teaching • Marketing • Accounting
Languages
Dutch — Native
English — C2
Japanese — A2
Interests
Surfing • Marathon (GAME) • History • UI/UX • Pokémon • Wine
Work Experience
Innodata
Outreach Consultant
Remote  |  2026
Driving strategic partnerships across university career centres and academic faculty in Japan to build a high-calibre talent pipeline for AI model evaluation and RLHF. Managing the entire contributor lifecycle; hosting technical info sessions to refining the onboarding of student researchers and subject matter experts.
One Coin English
ESL Teacher
Japan, Tokyo  |  2025
Taught private and group-based conversational English classes to Japanese professionals at beginner, intermediate and advanced levels. Provided trial lessons that resulted in strong conversion rates related to long-term enrolment.
Stichting Onderwijsconsulenten
Legislative Assistant
Netherlands, The Hague  |  2023
Contributed to the development of operational processes designed to enhance equitable access to education for neurodiverse students. Assisted in drafting and editing external policy documents. Managed financial administrative processes to ensure fiscal compliance.
ASN Bank
Communications Intern
Netherlands, The Hague  |  2022
Communications internship at the Dutch sustainable financial institution ASN Bank. Supported internal and external communications, content drafting, and editorial tasks.
